ranked_movies.title: The Descent
ranked_movies.reason: Highly recommended in the horror thread for its ability to induce fear, with multiple users praising its effectiveness in creating tension and terror, especially during the cave scenes.

ranked_movies.title: Hereditary
ranked_movies.reason: Cited as one of the scariest movies ever, with users emphasizing its psychological impact and the fact that it remains effective despite some viewers finding it overhyped.

ranked_movies.title: The Exorcist
ranked_movies.reason: Another classic horror film frequently mentioned as one of the scariest, often compared to Hereditary for its lasting impact on viewers.

ranked_movies.title: Spotlight
ranked_movies.reason: Highlighted in the investigative journalism thread as a strong example of a slow-burn thriller based on real events, similar to Zodiac.

ranked_movies.title: Zodiac
ranked_movies.reason: The central film in one of the threads, with users seeking similar investigative thrillers. It's praised for its detail-oriented approach and realism.

ranked_movies.title: The Secret Life of Walter Mitty
ranked_movies.reason: Mentioned as a comforting, feel-good movie that provides an escape from reality, with several users sharing personal stories of how it impacted them.

ranked_movies.title: The Princess Bride
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended as a book adaptation that stands on its own, with users noting it's a classic that doesn't require reading the book to enjoy.

ranked_movies.title: The Shining
ranked_movies.reason: Cited as a horror movie that remains effective even after multiple viewings, with one user mentioning they've seen it 30+ times and it still holds up.

ranked_movies.title: The Silence of the Lambs
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended in the investigative thread for its thriller elements and character depth, alongside its status as a classic.

ranked_movies.title: The Royal Tenenbaums
ranked_movies.reason: Mentioned in the comfort movies thread as a feel-good film, though it's also noted for its depth and humor.

ranked_movies.title: The Grand Budapest Hotel
ranked_movies.reason: A Wes Anderson film that received praise, though some users expressed disappointment in his recent work compared to this one.

ranked_movies.title: The French Dispatch
ranked_movies.reason: One of the later Wes Anderson films that divided fans, with some finding it too stylized and less substantive than his earlier work.

ranked_movies.title: The Father
ranked_movies.reason: Although not the main focus, it was mentioned in the context of adaptations and how some films stand alone.

ranked_movies.title: The Road
ranked_movies.reason: Mentioned as a book adaptation that works well as a film, though some users felt the book was better.

ranked_movies.title: The Royal Tenenbaums
ranked_movies.reason: Another Wes Anderson film that's praised for its heart and humor, often mentioned alongside The Grand Budapest Hotel.

ranked_movies.title: The Grand Budapest Hotel
ranked_movies.reason: Seen as a high point in Wes Anderson's career, with users noting it's been downhill since then in terms of emotional impact.

ranked_movies.title: The French Dispatch

ranked_movies.reason: A lesser-known Wes Anderson film that some users feel is underrated compared to his more famous works.

ranked_movies.reason: Another Wes Anderson film that divided fans, with some loving its whimsy and others finding it meandering.
